reviews.review_msg: the pop extra credit quizzes are a godsend. punnen is a decent prof, however his lectures are just him stating facts instead of actually teaching. his heavy accent, mixed with his messy handwriting, mixed with the fact he makes lots of errors, makes it difficult to follow along, definitely use lots of outside lecture time to grasp concepts fully

The other reviews are right. If you don't have a choice and have to take this course with him,practice every single week and go to office hours as much as possible. Not even close to the worst prof or course I've had, but it takes more work than it should. There are plenty of resources available online as well. Don't fall behind and you're gucci.

Moved the weight of our lowest midterm to the final, exams are direct copies from assignments (if you do bad its on you). Really easy final (taken from assignments and midterms). Accommodates well for students, really cares about his students learning/wellbeing. Really caring prof, who knows how to teach, would recommend taking it with him. :)

His self written "textbook" is horrible. His lectures are dense and hard to understand. His tests are the worst. Low class average. Too many questions packed into the 50 min limit. I think he's close to retirement age I don't think he cares anymore or maybe he never did.

He has is own textbook, but it's full with typos- it's annoying when you're practicing and you begin to question everything on there. he also has multiple select with multiple choice, which eats up a ton of time on the midterm. Grading scheme is also a pain if you're not good with exams- 90% exams, 10% homework. beware if you like to take your time
Can't understand him

Professor Abraham's method for math seems ineffective. Cancelling office hours before midterms,and suggesting emails for help, are impractical for math problems. The midterm, with 20 multiple-choice and 4 long-answer questions, didn't account for solving time An assignment is due post-midterm including exam topics.I'd recommend avoiding his classes

The first thing he tells you is not to listen to this website and that he is a "good teacher". DO NOT LISTEN and TAKE ANOTHER PROFESSOR. His notes alone deserve a 1 star rating. He had a quiet, challenging, passive personality that does not work with teaching a hard course. He withholds information and doesn't reveal solutions to homework.

He's not great at teaching but he's a good "teacher". I had to mostly self learn and it was hard since solutions aren't posted but I felt he cared about students and tried to make our life easier. He was lenient with grading as well and changed the course structure to 3 quizzes + project instead of a 40-50% final which I found extremely helpful.

That textbook he uses couldn't have gotten any worse. how can you put a bunch of examples 0 solutions? There is not even a clear explanation written in the book that you can follow to understand how to solve the question. He is such a bad prof, teaching math without writing anything on the board just like a philosophy class reading the slides

The lectures consist of him copy/pasting his textbook into slides and mumbling it off to the class. The notes are a mess. The tests are also extremely theory-based, so have fun remembering dozens of definitions and proofs. When the class average on our midterm was 40%, he proceeded to tell the class it was entirely our fault. Avoid at all costs.

Expected us to read the notes before class(ok)
Released notes for the week on Sunday(no time to read)
Notes were copied straight from the textbook 
Enough typos to make it hard to trust
Lectures were just going over examples already given to us 
very little reason to go to lectures.

In math teaching, visual component is vital. The prof is virtually not writing anything helpful during the post-midterm revision. You virtually cannot receive anything helpful from his pure-oral instruction.

The worst professor you will ever see. No replies to your email and his lectures are horrible. Don't ever take his classes and If you do, teach yourself if you wanna even pass. His notes are not useful and his assignments take forever and they are really hard. if you ever take his class you will hate yourself for that. stay away from him.
Online classes-- Unresponsive and unhelpful. No preparation or direction given before midterms or exams. Expect an entirely self-taught course.

Don't choose this professor. He doesn't do anything at all. His notes often skip steps or give only one final answer. The problem that should have been solved in 10 minutes will take you several hours.

He is the worst professors I've ever seen. In the online class, there are no videos or lectures (he only answers questions instead of giving notes. But he doesn't turn on the camera or share his screen, he just dictates complex questions by mouth.) He only posts notes, but these notes are very unclear and have many errors that he never fixes.
Dr. Punnen knows his subject well and teaches it clearly. Exams tended to be similar to the sample ones, so make sure you do those. He is a bit shy but will help you if you ask.

Punnen is great at explaining. He makes it very clear how to approach questions and goes through very thoroughly. Exams are do-able with hard work. Only complaint is it's hard to hear or see during lectures. I sat in the front and still could not see the tiny writing on whiteboard. He will not write bigger if you ask. People fought for front seats.

He's not too bad as a prof, he explains well and mainly uses examples to explain things. Do the assignments, they help. The biggest piece of advice possible is that you most likely WILL breeze easily through the first midterm BUT DON'T GET COCKY because the second one annihilates the class. Study like crazy for the second midterm! Or you'll cry :'(
goes super fast

He's not responsible at all. All the content from class is about concepts. No application ever went over in lectures. If you are good learner and more application learning type, dont take this prof. Teaching skill is good but no solution for sample tests, assignment marking scheme is weird.
Hammy is the best prof ever!!

If you keep up with the content it'll be no problem. Procasination is defintely a nono in this course especially if you have had no prior exposure to calculus. You can slip by the first midterm without doing work but on the second midterm it'll bite you in the tush.
abe's the best!!  easy class....but not all because content is easy...more because he is a good prof.
